degraded: F0 is RED with 3 failures, all environmental and all pre-existing: plugins/shipwright-security/tests/test_oss_backend_smoke.py needs semgrep, gitleaks and trivy on PATH, which this machine does not have -- the tests name the CI install step themselves. Verified by stashing the diff and re-running on the base tree, where they fail identically. The 6 test_combine_coverage failures reported earlier in this run are GONE: their cause was the worktree venv lacking the `coverage` module, fixed by provisioning it while repairing the SBOM license resolution. CI is 11/11 green on the runners, which settles the question empirically.

Gemini's external PLAN review leg was SUBSTANTIVE -- 5 coherent findings. That BREAKS the four-run pattern recorded on S2-S5 (four consecutive degenerate ~600-char truncations). Recorded because the earlier entries would otherwise read as a standing provider defect that has in fact not recurred. Both legs counted; two findings changed the implementation and two were resolved with a test rather than the suggested change.

SELF-INFLICTED, caught in code review, recorded because the internal notes were right while three SHIPPED documents were wrong. The ADR, the changelog drop and the migration guide all claimed 'the audit exit code is unchanged' and 'neither is a new block'. D2's flip IS a new block: `any_fail` is any(status == 'fail') with no detective-only exemption. The claim was true for D-layer and was carried across to D2 without re-reading the branch. All three corrected, and the guide is now checked against D2's actual verdict by a test so the two cannot drift apart again.

SELF-INFLICTED, second round, recorded because the FIRST correction of it was itself incomplete. The module written to stop D2 misdiagnosing an empty requirement set MISDIAGNOSED one: it branched on raw any_spec/rejects while its own docstring said to read the classification from group_i, and answered 'spec.md present but its requirements table holds no rows' for a spec whose rows are all RETIRED -- a file that plainly holds rows, which had been read, delivered as a non-zero exit. Group I had classified it correctly as all_rows_retired the whole time. Now consumes SpecScan.state/.detail and owns no classification. Separately, the exit-code correction shipped 2/3: the guide and changelog drop were fixed while ADR-109 went on asserting the retracted claim in Decision and Rationale, and listed 'escalate the empty set to a hard fail' as REJECTED while the diff implemented it for D2. The pin that was supposed to catch this read only ONE of the three documents, so it stayed green. It now reads all three and is verified to bite on the ADR.

Caught in review round 4, fixed before it could fire: the three-document pin hardcoded a path to a LIVE changelog drop under CHANGELOG-unreleased.d. aggregate_changelog unlinks every processed drop at release, so the next /shipwright-changelog run would have raised FileNotFoundError -- a run-time error, not an assertion with a message -- reddening the release PR from inside an unrelated plugin. It was the only test in the repo reading a committed drop by name; all 47 other references build drops under tmp_path. The changelog leg is now absence-tolerant with the two permanent legs kept strict and a checked>=2 floor, verified both ways empirically.

surface_verification.note: The user-facing surface of this change is the catalog document and the deep links that reach into it, so the suite drives the REAL chain rather than inspecting the file: it reads the generated traceability matrix, takes the link the matrix actually emits, resolves it relative to the matrix's own directory, opens whatever that lands on, and checks the fragment is defined there as an explicit anchor -- for EVERY one of the fifteen links. It also reads the catalog through BOTH production parsers (fr_table_reader and spec_parser.parse_fr_headings) to prove the fifteen per-requirement headings did not become a second parsed representation, and pins the one report about this catalog that is knowingly false. Verified NON-VACUOUS by re-running against the pre-change catalog: 15/15 deep links unresolved and 26 forbidden-token hits.

behaviors.behavior: all 15 requirement ids survive the merge, unrenumbered
the PRODUCTION parser (not a regex) still reads exactly 15 active rows with unchanged priorities -- the 15 new per-requirement headings did not become a second parsed representation
no requirement text (table Description or acceptance criterion) carries a run id, a decision-record number or a file path
every requirement has an explicit <a id=...> anchor, and every anchor is unique
EVERY deep link the generated traceability matrix emits resolves end to end: matrix -> relative link -> file on disk -> fragment defined there as an explicit anchor
the catalog did NOT move, and its path is covered by a registered, migrated artifact migration
the canon lint passes on the rewritten catalog
no Removed Requirements section is declared
every Layers cell keeps the literal (inferred) marker, so provenance stays legacy and no coverage gap hard-blocks
FV-2: D-layer stops claiming 'every active FR is covered' over an EMPTY requirement set and skips with a detail naming the state
FV-2 (corpus probe, subprocess-isolated): same flip observed through the harness
CONTROL: a populated manifest still reaches a real pass verdict -- the guard did not silence the coverage plane
BOUNDARY: 'every requirement retired' is NOT the empty case and keeps its pre-existing pass
FV-2: D2 fails when there are zero spec FRs but an event still references one -- the maximally-red state that was previously unreachable
FV-2 (corpus probe): same D2 flip observed through the harness
CONTROL: D2 still skips when there is genuinely nothing to compare
CONTROL: D2 still skips when there is no event log at all -- the guard that moved did not displace the one above it
CONTROL: D1/D2/D4 all still skip when nothing at all is present
DELIBERATE NON-FLIP: Group I keeps skipping on an empty set (detective-only; reddening it would be a false red)
DELIBERATE NON-FLIP: D-orphan keeps passing on an empty set (its sentence there is true, not vacuous)
I4 now FAILS on the same requirement id used in two different documents -- the externally visible behaviour change
CONTROL: distinct ids across two documents still pass -- multi-document is not itself the defect
CONTROL: a duplicate within one document still fails -- widening the scope dropped nothing
the renamed I4 finding says 'in the catalog', not 'within a split'
frozen_bugs records FV-2 as flipped with provenance, and STILL_FROZEN is now empty
the golden baseline is current with respect to the machinery (only the FV-2 metadata block moved; no discovery or parse matrix cell)
D2's new failure names the TABLE-SHAPE defect when a spec exists but every row was declined, instead of accusing the operator of stale references while naming the FRs the project has
CONTROL: the other empty state (no spec.md at all) keeps its own distinct diagnosis
CONTROL: with requirements present and readable, an unresolvable ref IS stale and keeps the historical wording

the collector does NOT short-circuit on an empty requirement set: zero requirements plus one @FR-tagged test yields a non-empty orphans list -- closing the falsifier S6 first shipped as an open argument

ALL THREE operator-facing documents (migration guide, changelog drop, ADR-109) match D2's actual verdict, checked against it rather than trusted -- the single-document version of this pin passed while the ADR still asserted the retracted claim. The ephemeral changelog leg tolerates its own absence (drops are consumed at release) while the two PERMANENT legs never may, and a floor asserts at least two documents were actually read.
the SECOND production parser over the catalog (spec_parser.parse_fr_headings) sees the same fifteen ids -- the coverage the old test name falsely implied
the FR-coherence report about this catalog is knowingly wrong (15/15 reported as missing description+acceptance) and the exact count is pinned so a fix forces the record to be updated
a spec whose FR rows are ALL RETIRED is not reported as an empty or broken table -- the case the first cut of the empty-state module got wrong by re-deriving a classification Group I already owned
the empty-state classifier's fail-soft path degrades to the historical wording exactly, so a future signature change cannot silently downgrade every D2 message with a green suite
the document pin survives the release that consumes its changelog drop, without the absence-tolerance degrading into tolerance of checking nothing
behaviors.evidence: plugins/shipwright-compliance/tests/test_group_d_empty_set_verdicts.py::test_every_operator_facing_document_matches_d2s_actual_verdict (checked>=2 floor; verified empirically by removing the drop -> passes, and by removing a permanent doc -> fails)

test_completeness.note: The consuming viewer's exact-anchor matching was originally listed here as an untestable behaviour. Removed on reflection rather than given a reason_code: it is a PREMISE of this change, not a behaviour this change delivers -- and it belongs to another repository's code. What this change delivers is the explicit anchor, which is the strictly stronger condition (it satisfies exact matching whatever the consumer's slug algorithm does), and that IS tested end to end. The premise is recorded under 'Honest limits' in ADR-109 where a reader will look for it. Seven behaviours were added in the review round: three for D2's misdiagnosis of a table-shape defect, one closing the D-orphan falsifier by probe, one tying the shipped migration guide to D2's actual verdict, and two pinning the SECOND parser over this catalog together with the false report it produces.
